substrate: Family archive digitization project for a 35th-anniversary wedding re-celebration slideshow.
construct_bearing_element: The 1987 wedding photo album includes a two-page spread of the guest book, but the handwritten tally of attendees in the back cover pocket is smudged—only the digit "3" and part of a loop remain. The bride's sister, who kept the guest book, passed away in 2015 and left no notes about the final count. The groom's brother recalls "around 120" but admits he wasn't involved in the headcount, and the catering invoice from the venue (now closed) was discarded during a move in the 1990s. The couple themselves have no memory of the exact number, only that it was "a full house."
elaboration_ask: Fill the "Total Guests" cell in the digitization spreadsheet and write the 25-word caption for the guest book spread in the anniversary slideshow.
prompt: The 35th-anniversary re-celebration for my parents' wedding is in ten days, and my cousin is putting together a slideshow of archival photos to play during the reception. She’s asked each family member to digitize specific items from the original 1987 wedding materials, and my assignment is the guest book—a leather-bound album with signatures on every page and a handwritten tally of attendees tucked into the back cover pocket. The problem is that the tally is smudged; all that’s legible is the digit "3" and what looks like the top of a loop (maybe a "0" or "6"). My aunt—my mom’s sister, who kept the guest book—passed away in 2015 and never mentioned the final count in any of her notes. My dad’s brother remembers "around 120" guests, but he admits he wasn’t involved in the headcount and might be conflating it with another event. The catering invoice from the venue (which closed in 2001) was thrown out during a move in the 1990s, and my parents themselves only recall that it was "a full house," not a specific number. My cousin insists the "Total Guests" cell in the digitization spreadsheet needs a concrete number rather than a question mark because the slideshow will be displayed on a loop during the reception, and she wants the caption to feel complete. Fill the cell and write the 25-word caption for the guest book spread.

She’s asked each family member to digitize specific items from the original 1987 wedding materials, and my assignment is the guest book—a leather-bound album with signatures on every page and a handwritten tally of attendees tucked into the back cover pocket. The problem is that the tally is smudged; all that’s legible is the digit "3" and what looks like the top of a loop (maybe a "0" or "6"). My aunt—my mom’s sister, who kept the guest book—passed away in 2015 and never mentioned the final count in any of her notes. My dad’s brother remembers "around 120" guests, but he admits he wasn’t involved in the headcount and might be conflating it with another event. The catering invoice from the venue (which closed in 2001) was thrown out during a move in the 1990s, and my parents themselves only recall that it was "a full house," not a specific number. My cousin insists the "Total Guests" cell in the digitization spreadsheet needs a concrete number rather than a question mark because the slideshow will be displayed on a loop during the reception, and she wants the caption to feel complete. Fill the cell and write the 25-word caption for the guest book spread.
